description: baseline failed because autoresearch runner could not spawn bash for ./autoresearch.sh

asi.hypothesis: Initial benchmark harness using a bash wrapper would establish baseline metrics for agent latency phases.
asi.error: run_experiment reported spawn bash ENOENT before executing ./autoresearch.sh.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark did not start; likely Windows environment lacks bash for run_experiment command wrapper or script shebang.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch autoresearch command to a direct Bun invocation or provide a Windows-compatible runner so run_experiment does not depend on bash.

description: baseline failed loading pi-natives native module under Windows Bun

asi.hypothesis: After making bash available to run_experiment, the benchmark would execute and produce baseline latency metrics.
asi.error: Bun could not resolve pi_natives.win32-x64.node from packages/natives/native/index.js; suggested rebuild or reinstall @oh-my-pi/pi-natives.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark process exited before metrics due missing native binding.
asi.next_action_hint: Avoid pi-natives-dependent imports in the benchmark or build packages/natives; easiest is to remove SearchTool/native grep and renderer imports that transitively load sanitizeText/pi-natives from the benchmark.

description: baseline for native-independent MCP tool-discovery latency workload

asi.hypothesis: Establish a stable baseline for pure MCP tool-discovery search, result formatting, and server summary latency before optimizing ranking algorithms.
asi.environment_note: run_experiment required a bash.exe shim in the user agent bin path on Windows; benchmark emits warnings about missing /tmp but completes successfully.
asi.bottleneck_hint: BM25 query time and repeated result formatting dominate the workload; both call searchDiscoverableMCPTools many times, so avoiding full sort/allocation for top-k should be promising.
